purpose: Define the promotion gates required before wake/listen, continuous voice, Realtime voice, or text-to-speech can become part of the governed Brand Doctor runtime.

requirements.label: Same runtime, evidence, and gates as typed turns
acceptanceCriteria: Voice turns emit the same evidence spotlight, memory, audit, and confirmation gates as typed turns.
requirements.nextAction: Keep every voice provider adapter bound to runAgentTurn before expanding voice modes.
guardrails: Voice must not bypass Brand Intelligence Packet, evidence, memory, audit, or confirmation gates.

requirements.label: Streaming status and canvas parity
acceptanceCriteria: Voice turns stream public status events.
Voice turns preserve the same ExperiencePlan canvas and approved dynamic views as typed turns.
requirements.nextAction: Continue routing voice-visible UI through ExperiencePlan and approved dynamic view IDs.
guardrails: Voice UI may summon approved views; it may not generate arbitrary unsupported UI.

requirements.label: Push-to-talk consent boundary
requirements.nextAction: Keep push-to-talk as the default consent pattern until privacy review approves broader listening.
guardrails: A visible user action is required before voice capture starts.

requirements.label: Browser speech prototype fallback
acceptanceCriteria: Unsupported browsers fall back to typed input.
blockers: Browser STT is client-only and not enterprise speech infrastructure.
requirements.nextAction: Treat browser STT as a demo convenience while provider and privacy requirements are reviewed.
guardrails: Do not position browser STT as production voice infrastructure.

requirements.label: Realtime voice unified with governed runtime
acceptanceCriteria: Realtime voice sessions call the same governed turn runtime.
Realtime events preserve ExperiencePlan, evidence spotlight, memory, audit, and gates.
blockers: Live Consult Realtime exists as a candidate endpoint but is not yet unified with runAgentTurn or ExperiencePlan output.
requirements.nextAction: Build a gated Realtime adapter that calls runAgentTurn and proves evidence/gate parity before enabling it in Agent Lab.
guardrails: Realtime voice cannot be enabled until it shares the governed runtime and proof rails.

requirements.label: Interruption and server-side cancellation
acceptanceCriteria: Client interruption preserves the last completed canvas.
blockers: Current recovery supports client stream abort only; server-side provider cancellation is not yet implemented.
requirements.nextAction: Define and test server-side cancellation semantics before enabling continuous or Realtime barge-in behavior.
guardrails: Continuous voice barge-in must not create overlapping runs or partially trusted canvases.

requirements.label: Continuous listening consent and privacy review
acceptanceCriteria: Data retention and transcript handling are documented.
blockers: Continuous voice mode is disabled by policy and capability flag until consent and privacy posture are reviewed.
requirements.nextAction: Complete consent, privacy, retention, and visible-state review before enabling wake/listen or continuous capture.
guardrails: No background wake-word or continuous listening behavior without explicit approval.

requirements.label: TTS provider and autonomous speaking policy
acceptanceCriteria: TTS provider is selected and gated.
Spoken output preserves caveats and human-review language.
blockers: Text-to-speech provider is not connected and autonomous speaking is disabled.
requirements.nextAction: Choose a TTS adapter and define spoken-output review rules before enabling assistant speech.
guardrails: Spoken output must preserve evidence caveats and must not convert options to commands.

requirements.label: Enterprise voice memory and transcript storage
acceptanceCriteria: Reviewed memory remains reversible and auditable.
blockers: Current persistence is local JSON prototype storage, not enterprise transcript or memory storage.
requirements.nextAction: Move reviewed memory, audit, artifacts, and voice transcript policy into enterprise storage design after governance review.
guardrails: Voice transcripts and memory must remain reviewable, reversible, and scoped to approved retention rules.
Full voice orchestration cannot bypass governed runtime, approved views, evidence spotlight, memory, audit, or confirmation gates.
Continuous listening, background wake word, Realtime voice, and TTS stay gated until their readiness requirements are ready.
Push-to-talk remains the default consent boundary.
caveats: This registry is a prototype promotion checklist, not final PepsiCo production voice policy.
Passing a readiness requirement does not enable a provider by itself; capability flags and runtime surface status still control activation.
